title: Quorum Queues, Raft Replication, Availability, and Data Safety · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective quorum queue safety and availability decision record; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in quorum queue safety and availability decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Choose quorum queues for durable replicated data safety and reject them for high-churn temporary queues, lowest latency, or extreme backlogs better served by streams.
Derive tolerated failures and availability from odd replica count, online majority, leader election, placement, and maintenance sequence.
Combine persistent messages, publisher confirms, manual consumer acknowledgements, and duplicate-safe applications for quorum data safety.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Majority Loss Recovery
steps.instruction:

content.text: A debug messaging team handles case 1314 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive majority availability and combine it with publisher and consumer data-safety evidence; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Majority Loss Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Automatic reconnect immediately restores a quorum queue that permanently lost its majority.
